What the hell happened at 10:30? — UB/ZB volume anomaly, 2026-07-14¶

Question (from the desk): at ~10:30 ET today, Ultra Bond (UB) and 30-Year Bond (ZB) futures traded more volume than during CPI — on a morning when June CPI (8:30 ET) was a shock −0.4% MoM print against ~50% priced odds of a July hike. What was it?

Timeline (ET), sources in data/headlines.json:

time event
08:30 June CPI −0.4% MoM, sharp downside surprise
10:00 Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's FIRST semiannual testimony begins (House Financial Services)
~10:25 Q&A begins; CNN: "Latest improvement on inflation isn't 'mission accomplished'"
10:34 the anomaly — peak 1-minute volume in UB & ZB (bigger than any CPI minute)
11:20 Trump drops the 20% Hormuz transit fee (Truth Social); oil off highs
11:50 Trump: strait open to all EXCEPT Iran (full blockade of Iranian ports)

Data: Massive futures REST 1-minute aggregates (front months UBU6/ZBU6/…, full session), 74 sessions of CBOT minute flat-files for baselines, Polygon 1-minute ETF bars, TLT tick tape with NBBO aggressor classification. Study directory: research/random-studies/unusual-trades/Fable/.

data loaded: ['6E', 'CL', 'ES', 'GC', 'TN', 'UB', 'ZB', 'ZF', 'ZN', 'ZT']

Synthesis — the numbers in one place¶

==============================================================================
TIMELINE (ET) — sources in data/headlines.json
  08:30  CPI: June -0.4% MoM (shock downside)
  10:00  Warsh testimony begins (House Financial Services)
  10:25  ~Q&A begins (approx)
  11:20  Trump drops 20% Hormuz protection fee (Truth Social)
  11:50  Trump: Hormuz open to all EXCEPT Iran; full blockade of Iranian ports
  12:21  Trump explains fee reversal to reporters
  12:49  Kuwait intercepts Iranian 'aerial targets'
==============================================================================
UB: event/CPI volume 0.67x | peak minute 10:34 | CPI move +16.6 bp vs event move +16.6 bp
ZB: event/CPI volume 0.69x | peak minute 10:34 | CPI move +22.6 bp vs event move +16.9 bp
==============================================================================

Verdict¶

The claim checks out at the minute scale. The 10:34 ET minute printed 11,958 UB and 21,132 ZB contracts — roughly 2× the biggest minute of today's CPI reaction (6,244 / 11,914 at 8:30) and bigger than the 8:30 peak minute of any CPI day in our 74-session sample. Over equal 15-minute windows CPI still wins (event window = 0.67–0.69× the CPI window): the anomaly was a single explosive minute, not a sustained wave.

What it was (from the tape):

  1. A buy-side sweep, instantly absorbed. The 10:34 candle in both UB and ZB is a huge upper wick — price probed ~10/32nds higher intraminute and closed back near the open. The TLT cash tape shows its largest net-buy minute of the morning (+230k shares, NBBO-classified) at exactly 10:34. Buyers hit through the book; sellers absorbed it within the minute.
  2. Long-end concentrated. Event-window volume tilt (event÷CPI relative volume) rises monotonically with maturity: ZT 0.39 → ZF 0.51 → ZN 0.48 → TN 0.67 → ZB 0.81 / UB 0.79. The CPI reaction was belly-led; the 10:30 flow specifically wanted duration.
  3. Macro, not idiosyncratic. In the event window the whole complex moved together — bonds +17 bp, ES +34 bp, gold +40 bp, crude −159 bp — and 1-minute bond-equity correlation rose (+0.16 to +0.25 across the curve). An isolated block/liquidation doesn't lift every asset's coupling.

Cause, by elimination: No coupon auction (bills only today, results 11:30), no Treasury buyback operation, Trump's Hormuz fee reversal posted 11:20 (that's the later move: bonds sold off hard from session highs 11:20→12:00 as the de-escalation risk-on hit). The burst lands 30–35 minutes into Warsh's first-ever testimony, at the start of Q&A, and its signature — instant two-way absorption, duration-seeking, risk-on-with-oil-down — reads as a headline-algo reaction to a dovish-read Warsh Q&A exchange stacked on a morning primed by the deflationary CPI shock (hike odds unwinding).

Caveats (data honesty): we could not retrieve a timestamped Q&A transcript (Bloomberg/CNN live blogs are paywalled), so "Warsh Q&A headline" is an inference from timing + cross-asset signature, not a quoted headline; futures tick/aggressor data is not available on this plan (minute aggregates only — TLT's NBBO tape is the aggressor lens); today's bars are REST-sourced (~15-min delayed feed, event fully covered) and the session was still open at pull time (14:30 ET).

Sources¶

  • BLS, June 2026 CPI release 2026-07-14 08:30 ET (schedule/archive)
  • Federal Reserve, Warsh semiannual testimony 2026-07-14
  • Bloomberg: Fed Rate-Hike Bets Mount Before Inflation Data, Warsh Testimony; live blog
  • CNN Business live updates: CPI + Warsh testimony
  • CBS News live updates: US–Iran / Strait of Hormuz (fee reversal 11:32 EDT entry)
  • CNBC: Oil off highs after Trump abandons 20% Hormuz fee; watch Warsh testify
  • Al Jazeera: Oil hits 1-month high as US-Iran fighting clouds Hormuz outlook
  • Mediaite: Trump backs down on 20% Hormuz fee
  • Market data: Massive (futures REST aggs + S3 minute flat-files), Polygon (equity aggs, trades, NBBO)
